design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate multi-screen spatial puzzles using realistic human movement (jump, climb, grab ledges) while managing limited resources (ammo, shield charge). Solve environmental pathfinding to progress through seven levels.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: High fall damage and realistic weakness create spatial consequence. Shield as health pool creates resource tension. Spatial puzzle discovery creates navigation tension.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as amnesiac intelligence agent unraveling alien conspiracy through investigation, navigation, and combat. Experience cinematic, fluid animation storytelling.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commitment to rotoscoped, hand-drawn animation creating fluid human movement. Rejection of artificial platformer abilities—Conrad moves at realistic human speed with realistic jumping limits and vulnerability to falls. Multi-screen non-scrolling level design forces spatial reasoning. Late-game introduction of teleportation device adds problem-solving dimensionality without abandoning core philosophy.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Spatial puzzle discovery requires trial-and-error navigation; player must learn screen layouts and coordinate jumps across multiple altitude levels. Shield provides buffer against enemy fire but requires strategic positioning. Realistic fall damage punishes careless jumping.

progression.upgrade_system: Progression through equipment: pistol with unlimited ammo (starting), force shield (health system), portable force field (temporary enemy shot barrier), teleportation device (late-game spatial puzzle solving enabler)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Level-based progression. Equipment unlocks tied to story progression. Late-game teleportation device enables access to previously unreachable areas, creating backtracking potential.
replayability_hooks: Multi-screen spatial layouts reward mastery of navigation
Rotoscoped animation aesthetic creates cinematic replay value
Seven distinct level environments with escalating spatial complexity

world_building_techniques: Environmental exploration of alien-infiltrated city (New Washington, Titan planet)
Gladiatorial 'Death Tower' tournament as narrative checkpoint
Eavesdropping on Morphs through vent (cinematic discovery)
Multi-level hideout infiltration revealing alien conspiracy structure

faction_indicators: Corrupt police attacking innocent civilians (New Washington authorities)
Morph infiltrators disguised as human authority figures
Human prisoner Phillip Howard Clark held captive by Morphs
Gladiatorial 'Death Tower' tournament as survival-of-fittest spectacle
danger_signals: Amnesiac protagonist waking with no memory (sign of forced neural alteration)
Morphs erasing evidence of their existence from human minds
Infiltrators prepared for agent's return to Earth
Teleportation technology to alien homeworld (existential threat scale)
environmental_storytelling: Jungle terrain on Titan planet (off-world colonization)
Multi-screen city architecture of New Washington (futuristic urban design)
Vertical level design with multiple altitude changes (3D spatial representation)
Vent systems and underground passages revealing hidden Morph infrastructure
Nightclub facade concealing alien conspiracy headquarters

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Flashback: The Quest for Identity (SNES)
